description: The scanning electron micrograph shows the surface morphology of a Fe-7.5Mn-5Al alloy after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hours in pure oxygen at 200 torr, displaying both large and small oxide nodules. The field of view is dominated by a central large, cauliflower-like nodule with a rough textured surface, surrounded by numerous smaller spherical nodules dispersed across the oxidized surface. The scale bar indicates 130 μm. According to the caption and cited text, this alloy composition lies at the critical transition boundary near 7.5% manganese, where both nodular types coexist. The large nodules are reported to comprise an outer Fe2O3 layer and an inner (Fe,Mn)3O4 layer, while the small nodules possess a grey manganese oxide layer at the gas-oxide interface above a white Fe2O3 layer. This figure provides direct visual evidence that the transition from large iron-rich nodules to small manganese-rich nodules occurs at approximately 7.5% Mn, supporting the core conclusion that manganese concentration governs the scale morphology and the shift from iron-dominated to manganese-dominated oxidation behavior.
